evals.query: Create a Kora JDBC repository for a User with an auto-generated Long id, email, name and createdAt columns. I need findById, findAll, insert and delete.
expected_behavior: Defines a record annotated with @EntityJdbc (from ru.tinkoff.kora.database.jdbc), @Table, and @Column/@Id from ru.tinkoff.kora.database.common.annotation
Declares a @Repository interface that extends JdbcRepository
Uses SQL macros such as %{return#selects}, %{return#table}, %{entity#inserts} in @Query
Returns @Nullable User (preferred over Optional) for findById and List<User> for findAll
Adds the annotation processor (annotationProcessor "ru.tinkoff.kora:annotation-processors") and a JDBC driver dependency
Does not use JPA @Entity, reflection-based ORM annotations, or field injection

evals.query: How do I insert a row and get back the database-generated identity id in a Kora JDBC repository?
expected_behavior: Annotates the insert method with @Id and returns the id type (e.g. Long)
Excludes the id column from the INSERT using %{entity#inserts-= @id} or a RETURNING id clause
Notes that @Id-on-method also works with @Batch inserts returning List<Long>
Does not invent a non-existent %{entity#deletes} macro or a @GeneratedValue annotation

evals.query: Write a Kora service that inserts an order and reserves inventory atomically, sending a notification only after the transaction commits.
expected_behavior: Runs the work inside JdbcConnectionFactory.inTx(), obtained via repository.getJdbcConnectionFactory() or injected JdbcConnectionFactory
Calls multiple repository methods inside the same inTx lambda so they share one transaction and roll back together on exception
Registers the notification with currentConnectionContext().addPostCommitAction(...)
Declares the service as a @Component with constructor injection
Does not use a @Transactional annotation or declarative transaction propagation

evals.query: Add bulk insert and update methods to my Kora JDBC UserRepository.
expected_behavior: Annotates the List<User> parameter with @Batch
Returns UpdateCount (or List of generated ids) rather than the entities, since batch queries cannot project arbitrary rows
Uses %{entity#inserts} / %{entity#updates} macros in @Query
Does not use PreparedStatement.addBatch by hand or an external JdbcTemplate helper

evals.query: My Kora entity has a Status enum stored as an integer code column. How do I map it both ways?
expected_behavior: Implements JdbcResultColumnMapper<Status> with apply(ResultSet rs, int index) to read the code
Implements JdbcParameterColumnMapper<Status> with set(PreparedStatement, int, value) to write the code
Selects both mappers by repeating @Mapping(...) on the @Column field (not @Tag, not an array literal)
Does not use a JPA AttributeConverter or an external converter framework

evals.query: I have an order_items table with a composite primary key (order_id, product_id). How do I model it in Kora JDBC?
expected_behavior: Models the key as a nested record referenced with @Id @Embedded on the parent entity field
Uses %{id#where} in findById/deleteById queries to match all key columns
Keeps @EntityJdbc on the entity (and lets the embedded id flatten into columns)
Does not use JPA @IdClass or @EmbeddedId

evals.query: How do I persist a nested record into a PostgreSQL jsonb column with Kora JDBC?
expected_behavior: Annotates the field with @Json and provides generic @Json JdbcParameterColumnMapper / JdbcResultColumnMapper via a small @Module that depends on JsonWriter/JsonReader
Casts the parameter to jsonb in SQL, e.g. VALUES (:entity.value::jsonb)
Wires the JSON module (JsonModule/JsonCommonModule) into the @KoraApp graph
Does not use Jackson ObjectMapper directly or an external JdbcTemplate RowMapper

evals.query: Configure the HikariCP connection pool for a Kora JDBC service with credentials from environment variables and a 20-connection pool.
expected_behavior: Places jdbcUrl, username, password, poolName and maxPoolSize under the db section read by JdbcDatabaseConfig
Uses HOCON duration strings like connectionTimeout = "10s" rather than millisecond numbers
Substitutes credentials with environment variables (${POSTGRES_JDBC_URL}, ${POSTGRES_USER}, ${POSTGRES_PASS})
Wires JdbcDatabaseModule into @KoraApp and a config module (HoconConfigModule)
Does not configure a manual DataSource bean or external datasource property keys

evals.query: How should I write an integration test for a Kora JDBC repository against a real PostgreSQL?
expected_behavior: Uses @KoraAppTest with test-junit5 and injects the repository with @TestComponent
Starts PostgreSQL via a Testcontainers extension and points the config at it (e.g. KoraConfigModification system properties)
Exercises the real generated repository implementation rather than instantiating it by hand
Does not use a full-application boot test annotation from another framework, a JPA slice test, or mock the repository
